symptom: Photo shows silver-colored solid branch-circuit conductors on receptacles, switches, wirenuts, or breakers in an older dwelling.
diagnosis: Likely solid aluminum branch-circuit wiring. Main risk is heat from oxidation, creep, and devices or splices not rated for aluminum conductors.
procedure: Confirm conductor material by color, markings, and age of installation.
Kill power and verify dead before opening or moving the device.
Check whether devices are marked CO/ALR where aluminum terminates directly.
Inspect for heat discoloration, brittle insulation, loose screws, or damaged wirenuts.
Use approved aluminum-to-copper repair connectors or escalate for remediation.
safety_notes: Do not land aluminum conductors on devices rated copper-only.
Do not use standard wirenuts as a repair unless the connector is listed for the conductor combination.
If there are heat marks or melted insulation, stop and treat as a fire-risk repair.
